topic: Agentic Workflow SOP — core.py editing speed
content:
RULE: When editing core.py on Claude Desktop, use this decision tree:
1. Change is 1-2 lines, no emoji/em-dash → try gh_search_replace once. If fails → go to step 2 immediately, never retry.
2. Change involves unicode, emoji, em-dash, or multi-location → skip gh_search_replace entirely. Go straight to PowerShell full-file read-modify-write. One tool call beats 3 failed attempts.
3. Multi-location changes in same file → batch ALL into one PowerShell read-modify-write. Never do sequential patches.
4. After any PowerShell line-splice → always read back the 5 lines around the edit to verify syntax (missing cid), unclosed strings etc).
5. Reading context before edit → use gh_read_lines with tight range (5-10 lines), never read 50+ lines for a known small change.
TARGET: Any single core.py fix should be 2-3 tool calls max: read target lines → patch → verify.

topic: core.py editing speed SOP
content: core.py edit decision tree: (1) simple ASCII change: gh_search_replace once, if fails go to PowerShell immediately. (2) unicode/emoji/em-dash/multi-location: skip gh_search_replace, use PowerShell full-file read-modify-write directly. (3) multi-location same file: batch ALL changes in one PS read-modify-write, never sequential patches. (4) after any line-splice: read back 5 surrounding lines to verify syntax. (5) context reads: gh_read_lines tight range 5-10 lines only. TARGET: 2-3 tool calls per fix max.

topic: zapier_mcp_overview
content:
ZAPIER MCP — CORE AGI External Action Layer

Config URL: https://mcp.zapier.com/mcp/servers/1b142a72-dda7-4341-a414-0a55cc6ee99f/config
MCP URL: https://mcp.zapier.com/mcp/servers/1b142a72-dda7-4341-a414-0a55cc6ee99f/mcp

MENTAL MODEL: Zapier MCP is a curated action registry — NOT a general HTTP client. Ki enables specific actions at the config URL. Each enabled action becomes zapier:<app>_<action> MCP tool. Claude calls it with instructions (plain English) + named params. Zapier handles all auth. No Zaps needed.

Flow: CORE AGI → zapier MCP tool → Zapier action (pre-authed) → 3rd party app (8000+ available)

BOTTLENECK: Not capability but which actions Ki has enabled. Enable more = CORE can do more.

TO ADD NEW ACTIONS: config URL → search app → select actions → save → immediately available.
Rule: Enable only what CORE needs. 5-15 focused actions per domain. Too many = Claude picks wrong one.

CURRENTLY ENABLED (2026-03-12):
zapier:google_drive_retrieve_files_from_google_drive, zapier:google_drive_delete_file,
zapier:google_drive_delete_file_permanent, zapier:google_sheets_delete_spreadsheet_row_s,
zapier:google_sheets_delete_sheet, zapier:google_sheets_api_request_beta,
zapier:google_docs_api_request_beta, zapier:google_drive_api_request_beta,
zapier:google_slides_api_request_beta, zapier:github_submit_review, zapier:get_configuration_url

USAGE RULES:
- instructions field: plain English, be specific especially for writes/deletes
- output_hint (REQUIRED): always supply — "just the file ID and name" / "confirm success only"
- Without output_hint: Zapier returns large raw JSON blobs that waste context window
- For critical writes: supply named params directly, don't rely on instructions inference alone
- No triggers — only actions. App→CORE flows still need a real Zap with trigger.
- Rate limits apply — don't loop over Zapier calls in bulk

topic: zapier_connections_p0
content:
ZAPIER CONNECTIONS — Priority P0 (Connect First, Week 1)

These 8 connections unlock CORE's most critical blind spots. CORE is partially blind without them.

1. GMAIL (zapier:gmail_send_email, find_email, create_draft, reply_to_email)
   Why: Primary backup alert channel. Telegram is single point of failure. Send session summaries,
   error reports, milestone alerts to Ki. Indonesia-accessible.
   Ki must do: Connect Google account to Zapier (OAuth).

2. NOTION (zapier:notion_create_page, update_page, find_page, create_database_item, update_database_item, query_database)
   Why: CORE's human-readable external knowledge base. Milestones, evolution logs, project docs.
   Supabase handles internal memory — Notion handles structured knowledge Ki can browse.
   Ki must do: Create Notion workspace with "CORE Sessions" and "CORE Milestones" databases.
   Connect Notion to Zapier. Share workspace with Zapier integration.

3. TODOIST (zapier:todoist_create_task, update_task, complete_task, find_task)
   Why: Human-facing task queue. CORE queues work here when Ki needs to act manually.
   Ki must do: Connect Todoist account to Zapier.

4. PERPLEXITY (zapier:perplexity_ask_question)
   Why: Real-time web research. CORE can query anything without a browser. Currently CORE
   has zero external information perception beyond Claude's training cutoff.
   Ki must do: Get Perplexity API key (paid tier required for Zapier).

5. BETTER UPTIME (zapier:betteruptime_create_monitor)
   Why: Self-monitoring. CORE needs to know Railway is down before Ki wakes up.
   Monitor: https://core-agi-production.up.railway.app/health every 5 min.
   Ki must do: Create free Better Uptime account. Set Ki's email/phone as alert target.

6. WEBHOOKS BY ZAPIER (zapier:webhooks_post, webhooks_get)
   Why: Escape hatch — call ANY URL. Railway /patch endpoint, custom services, anything.
   Requires no account, zero cost, maximum flexibility.
   Ki must do: Just enable in Zapier config. No account needed.

7. GOOGLE CALENDAR (zapier:google_calendar_create_event, find_event, update_event, delete_event)
   Why: CORE needs time awareness. Schedule maintenance windows, review sessions, milestone reviews.
   Ki must do: Connect Google Calendar to Zapier. Create "CORE AGI" calendar to isolate CORE events.

8. GOOGLE DRIVE WRITE ACTIONS (expand existing connection)
   Already connected for reads/deletes. Add: upload_file, create_folder, create_file_from_text, copy_file
   Why: CORE generates reports, session summaries, outputs — needs to store them in Drive.
   Ki must do: Expand existing Google Drive Zapier connection to include write actions.
   Create "CORE AGI" folder with subfolders: Sessions/, Reports/, Evolutions/, Artifacts/

DOMAIN 7 — FILES & DOCS: Google Drive, Google Docs, Google Sheets, Dropbox
  Purpose: Store CORE outputs humans can read. Reports, session logs, artifacts.

DOMAIN 8 — CODE & DEPLOY: GitHub write actions, Vercel, Cloudflare webhook
  Purpose: CORE self-modifies. Backup write path beyond gh_search_replace.

DOMAIN 9 — SOCIAL & WEB: Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram, Reddit, Medium
  Purpose: Manage Ki's public presence. Post CORE milestones.

DOMAIN 10 — SCHEDULING: Google Calendar, Calendly
  Purpose: Time awareness. Schedule maintenance windows, review sessions.

DOMAIN 11 — COMMERCE: Stripe, Gumroad, Shopify, Midtrans (Indonesia)
  Purpose: Monitor revenue if Ki sells anything. Midtrans most relevant locally.

DOMAIN 12 — AI TOOLS: OpenAI, Gemini, ElevenLabs, Stability AI
  Purpose: Multi-model routing. Fallback reasoning, image gen, audio output.

DOMAIN 13 — IOT & PHYSICAL: Home Assistant, IFTTT, Philips Hue, Oura Ring, Strava
  Purpose: CORE interacts with Ki's physical environment. Biometrics awareness.

DOMAIN 14 — CRM & PEOPLE: HubSpot, Airtable, Google Contacts, Clay
  Purpose: Relationship intelligence. Track collaborators, mentors, contacts.

DOMAIN 15 — WEBHOOKS (ESCAPE HATCH): Webhooks by Zapier, Code by Zapier
  Purpose: Call ANY URL. Execute code. Maximum flexibility. Requires no account.
  MOST POWERFUL single addition — enable this first.

DOMAIN 16 — HEALTH & LIFE: Strava, Oura, MyFitnessPal, Daylio, Apple Health
  Purpose: Wellbeing awareness. CORE adapts to Ki's physical/mental state.

DOMAIN 17 — MEDIA & CONTENT: YouTube, Spotify, Pocket, Readwise, Kindle
  Purpose: Curate what Ki learns. Surface relevant material for current tasks.

BEFORE vs AFTER (key capability gaps Zapier closes):
  Alert channels:  Telegram only → + Gmail + Slack + WhatsApp
  Memory:          Supabase only → + Notion + Google Docs (human-readable)
  Self-monitoring: Manual checks → PagerDuty + Better Uptime auto-alerts
  Research:        Claude training only → + Perplexity + RSS real-time
  Scheduling:      None → Google Calendar events
  Cost tracking:   None → Sheets + Wave ledger
  Other AI models: Groq only → + OpenAI + Gemini + ElevenLabs

topic: binance_pay_merchant_api
content:
BINANCE PAY — CORE AGI Revenue Layer (Merchant API)

Binance Pay lets CORE accept crypto payments. Strongest in Indonesia/SEA. Zero tx fees. 200+ currencies.
Merchant portal: https://merchant.binance.com/en
API base: https://bpay.binanceapi.com
Auth: HMAC-SHA512 — timestamp + nonce + body signed with secret key

KEY ENDPOINTS:
- POST /binancepay/openapi/v3/order — create payment order (v3 is current, v2 deprecated)
- POST /binancepay/openapi/v2/order/query — check order status
- POST /binancepay/openapi/v3/order/close — cancel order
- POST /binancepay/openapi/v2/refund — issue refund
- POST /binancepay/openapi/transfer — C2C send crypto to another user
- POST /binancepay/openapi/direct-debit/contract — create subscription (whitelist required)
- POST /binancepay/openapi/payout/transfer — bulk payouts
- GET /binancepay/openapi/balance — check merchant wallet balance
- GET /binancepay/openapi/certificates — get public key for webhook verification

PAYMENT FLOW:
1. CORE calls create order → gets prepayId + checkoutUrl + qrContent
2. Ki shows customer: QR code (scan in Binance app) or checkoutUrl (web redirect)
3. Customer pays → Binance fires webhook to /webhook/binance-pay
4. CORE verifies signature → marks paid in Supabase → sends Telegram alert → returns HTTP 200 + "SUCCESS"
5. Binance retries up to 6 times if no HTTP 200

CREATE ORDER v3 REQUIRED FIELDS: env.terminalType, merchantTradeNo (unique 32 char), description (NEW in v3), goodsDetails[], and either (orderAmount+currency) or (fiatAmount+fiatCurrency)

WEBHOOK SECURITY (critical):
- Get public key from GET /binancepay/openapi/certificates (cache it)
- Payload: timestamp + "\n" + nonce + "\n" + rawBody + "\n"
- Verify: base64_decode(BinancePay-Signature header) against SHA256 with RSA PKCS1v15
- Idempotency: merchantTradeNo is dedup key — same webhook can arrive multiple times
- bizStatus values: PAY_SUCCESS, PAY_FAIL, PAY_CLOSED, REFUND_SUCCESS

AUTHENTICATION HEADER PATTERN:
BinancePay-Timestamp: epoch ms
BinancePay-Nonce: random 32-char alphanumeric
BinancePay-Certificate-SN: API_KEY
BinancePay-Signature: hmac-sha512(timestamp+\n+nonce+\n+body+\n).upper()

CREDENTIALS (in Cloudflare vault):
BINANCE_PAY_API_KEY — from merchant.binance.com → Developers → API Keys
BINANCE_PAY_SECRET_KEY — same (shown once at creation)

Ki must do:
1. Apply at merchant.binance.com (KYC, individual merchant OK, 1-3 day approval)
2. Generate API keys after approval
3. Set webhook URL: https://core-agi-production.up.railway.app/webhook/binance-pay
4. Store credentials in Cloudflare vault
5. Add /webhook/binance-pay endpoint + create_payment_order() function to core.py

topic: binance_agent_skills
content:
BINANCE AI AGENT SKILLS — CORE AGI Intelligence + Trading Layer

Source: https://github.com/binance/binance-skills-hub (official Binance repo, launched Q1 2025)
Same SKILL.md format as CORE's skill system. Works with any AI framework including Claude.
7 skills total. Skills 2-7 are PUBLIC APIs — no auth, callable today via web_fetch.
Only Skill 1 (Spot trading) requires API keys.

SKILL 1 — BINANCE SPOT (CEX Trading)
Auth: YES — API Key + Secret Key (IP-restricted to Railway)
Can do: Real-time market data (ticker, order book, candlesticks, exchange info)
Can do: Execute trades (market order, limit order, OCO, OPO, OTOCO)
Can do: Cancel orders, check order status, get account balances, trade history
CRITICAL RULE: Always require user to type "CONFIRM" before any mainnet trade
Testnet available: testnet.binance.vision (use for all testing first)
Credentials: BINANCE_API_KEY, BINANCE_SECRET_KEY (spottrading + read permissions, NO withdrawals)

SKILL 2 — QUERY ADDRESS INFO (Wallet/Whale Tracking)
Auth: NO (public) | Chains: BNB Chain, Ethereum, others
Can do: Analyze any wallet — holdings breakdown, total valuation, 24h changes, concentration
Use case: Track "smart money" wallets. Monitor when whales accumulate.
API base: https://web3.binance.com/bapi/defi/v1/public/...

SKILL 3 — QUERY TOKEN INFO (Token Due Diligence)
Auth: NO (public)
Can do: Token metadata, price, market cap, liquidity pool depth, top holders, trading volume
Use case: Run before buying ANY token. Instant due diligence snapshot.

SKILL 4 — CRYPTO MARKET RANK (Market Intelligence)
Auth: NO (public)
Can do: Trending tokens, smart money inflow rankings, social hype scores, top trader PnL,
        Binance Alpha tokens, hot searches, meme narratives
Use case: Daily morning briefing — "what's hot today, what is smart money buying"
Key endpoint: https://web3.binance.com/bapi/defi/v1/public/wallet-direct/buw/wallet/market/token/pulse/unified/rank/list

SKILL 5 — MEME RUSH (Meme Token Monitoring)
Auth: NO (public)
Can do: Track meme token creation on BNB Chain, migration events, top 100 by breakout score
        Narrative stages: launch → hype → peak → dump
Use case: Catch meme coins early. CORE monitors 24/7, alerts when breakout pattern starts.
Key endpoint: https://web3.binance.com/bapi/defi/v1/public/wallet-direct/buw/wallet/market/token/pulse/exclusive/rank/list

SKILL 6 — SMART MONEY SIGNALS (Signal Intelligence)
Auth: NO (public)
Can do: Large wallet buy/sell signals in real-time, filter by chain/token/wallet type,
        smart money inflow/outflow by token, identify accumulation vs distribution
Use case: Never trade against smart money. CORE alerts when institutional wallets move.

SKILL 7 — CONTRACT RISK DETECTION (Security Audit)
Auth: NO (public)
Can do: Honeypot detection, mint/freeze authority check, ownership concentration,
        buy/sell tax analysis, liquidity lock status, common rug pull patterns
Use case: MANDATORY before buying any new token. CORE auto-audits on request.

INTEGRATION PATTERN FOR CORE:
- Intelligence skills (2-7): use web_fetch directly in Claude Desktop → no code changes needed
- Spot trading (skill 1): needs Python function in core.py + API keys in Cloudflare vault
- Skills can be triggered via Telegram commands or Claude Desktop conversation

TODAY'S CAPABILITY (zero setup): Audit tokens, check market trends, analyze wallets, monitor memes
AFTER SETUP: Full portfolio management, automated trade execution with confirmation gate

CREDENTIALS (in Cloudflare vault):
BINANCE_API_KEY — from binance.com → Profile → API Management → Create API
BINANCE_SECRET_KEY — same (shown once)
Permissions: Enable Reading + Enable Spot Trading + IP restriction to Railway IP
Do NOT enable: Withdrawals, Futures, Margin
Testnet (optional): BINANCE_TESTNET_KEY, BINANCE_TESTNET_SECRET from testnet.binance.vision

RISK RULES (hard limits):
- Never trade without CONFIRM from Ki
- Max 10% of portfolio per single trade
- Spot only — no leverage, no margin, no futures
- Audit every new token before buying
- Testnet first for any new trading code

- Customer Comments / RFIs → /JK1-2 Equinix DC CLX - Comments by Customer, /JK1-2 Equinix DC MLTX - Comments by Customer
- MOPs & JSAs → /JK1-2 MOP & JSA

Notes: JK1-1 is marked as REFERENCE (likely previous phase or baseline docs).

topic: DAILY_REPORT_FORMAT: JK1-2 Equinix DC
content:
DAILY REPORT FORMAT — JK1-2 Equinix DC (LSEI)
Source: Daily_Report_10Mar2026.docx ~ 13Mar2026.docx + SESSION_NOTES.md

=== FILE NAMING ===
Daily_Report_[DD][Mon][YYYY].docx
Example: Daily_Report_10Mar2026.docx

=== DOCUMENT TITLE ===
"Daily Report - [Weekday], [DD Month YYYY]"
Example: "Daily Report - Tuesday, 10 March 2026"

=== 4 SECTIONS (exact order) ===

1. Site Activities
2. Document & Submission Progress
3. Issues / Constraints
4. Plan for Next Day

=== SECTION RULES ===
- Pure bullet points — NO tables, NO timestamps/jam, NO decorative elements
- Email-ready format (copy-paste friendly)
- Written in MIXED language: Indonesian primary, English for technical terms
  (equipment codes, doc names, CxL levels, status labels stay in English)
- NO section intro paragraph — go straight to bullets

=== SECTION 1: Site Activities ===
- Starts with "Morning briefing" bullet always
- Activities listed chronologically through the day
- Each bullet = one activity (supervisi, monitoring, submit, review, koordinasi)
- Technical equipment codes stay in English: 1MLTX_B, 1RMU_B, 1MSB_B_1, etc.
- CxLevel notation: CxL2A, CxL2B, CxL3
- Stakeholder names: Sygna/CTA (CxA), Atoz (GC), LSEI (our team), Equinix Ops
- Ends with recap/wrap-up bullet (update tracker, koordinasi permit, kirim update ke klien)

=== SECTION 2: Document & Submission Progress ===
- Format per bullet: "[Doc Name] - [STATUS]: [brief description]"
- STATUS labels (caps): SUBMITTED, IN PROGRESS, AWAITING REVIEW, APPROVED, FINAL REVIEW, CLOSED, UNDER REVIEW, COMPLETED, DRAFT, FOLLOW-UP
- Documents tracked: MOS CxL2A/B/C, ITP, PTW, MOM, Sertifikat Kalibrasi, TWLA

=== SECTION 3: Issues / Constraints ===
- Problem bullets — plain, no numbering
- Each issue = one bullet, brief explanation + action taken or next step
- Common issue types: site access delay, punch list open, document awaiting response, resource constraint

=== SECTION 4: Plan for Next Day ===
- Action items for next working day
- Format: verb + object + detail (Submit X ke Y, CxL2B Z - koordinasi CTA, Follow-up A)
- If Friday → Plan for Next Day covers Monday, prefixed: "Senin [DD] Mar:"

=== REPORTER IDENTITY ===
Position: Manager Engineering & Service — LS Electric Indonesia (LSEI)
Role in report: koordinasi, supervisi, submit dokumen — not hands-on technician

=== CONTINUITY RULES ===
- Issues carry over day to day until CLOSED
- Document statuses progress logically (DRAFT → IN PROGRESS → SUBMITTED → UNDER REVIEW → APPROVED/CLOSED)
- TWLA (Two-Week Look Ahead) is the schedule reference — reference it in briefing and planning bullets

=== ACTIVE PROJECT CONTEXT (as of 13 Mar 2026) ===
- MOS CxL2B MLTX R3 — UNDER REVIEW by Sygna
- MOS CxL2A MLTX R1 — AWAITING REVIEW (overdue, escalation pending)
- MOS CxL3 MLTX — IN PROGRESS, internal draft target 17 Mar
- Sertifikat kalibrasi tools — CLOSED (submitted 12 Mar)
- TWLA 16-22 Mar — to be aligned with GC on Monday

=== WHATSAPP REPORT STYLE (informal channel) ===
- Shorter, casual tone, bahasa Indonesia
- Mentions specific equipment touched (RMU B, MLTX B)
- Ends with "terima kasih 🙏🏻"
- Used for same-day quick updates to Pak Vega, Rifqi, Syarif

topic: WEEKLY_REPORT_GENERATION_METHODOLOGY: JK1-2 Equinix DC
content:
WEEKLY REPORT GENERATION METHODOLOGY — From Daily Report Data

=== GOAL ===
Auto-generate weekly report PPTX (same format as E&S Team boss template) by extracting and synthesizing data from daily report docx files + reference files.

=== DATA SOURCES (update regularly) ===
1. Daily Reports: F:\...\Daily Report\Daily_Report_[DD][Mon][YYYY].docx
   → Primary source of "what happened this week"
   → Extract: Site Activities, Doc Progress, Issues, Next Plans

2. TWLA Schedule: referensi\JK1-2 Cx TWLA Schedule 9-22 March 2026.xlsx
   → Source of "what was planned" vs "what got done"
   → Structure: Date rows × Level columns (Level 2-7, Roof)
   → Used to: compare planned vs actual, identify slippage

3. Equipment List: referensi\JK1-2 EQUINIX DC Equipment Supply List - LSEI.xlsx
   → Equipment registry: RMU_B/C/D/F, MLTX_B/C/D/F, BTU, Control Box
   → Used to: validate equipment codes, assign levels

4. MOM / CxL Reports (PDF): referensi\MOM CX WEEKLY MEETING*.pdf, REPORT CXL*.pdf
   → Issues raised in weekly meetings, CxL status per equipment
   → Used to: populate issues section, confirm doc status

5. WhatsApp log: referensi\whatsapp report chat.txt
   → Informal field updates — supplement Site Activities with ground truth

=== EXTRACTION SCHEMA FROM DAILY REPORTS ===

For each Daily_Report_[date].docx, extract into structured data:

A) SITE_ACTIVITIES[]
   - date: DD Mon YYYY
   - items: list of activity strings
   - equipment_touched: list of equipment codes (e.g. 1MLTX_B, 1RMU_F)
   - cx_level: CxL level performed (CxL2A, CxL2B, CxL3)
   - location_level: Level 2-7

B) DOC_PROGRESS[]
   - doc_name: string (e.g. "MOS CxL2B MLTX R3")
   - status: SUBMITTED | IN PROGRESS | AWAITING REVIEW | APPROVED | CLOSED | etc.
   - detail: brief description

C) ISSUES[]
   - issue: string description
   - status: OPEN | CLOSED
   - carry_over: boolean (same issue as previous day)

D) NEXT_PLAN[]
   - action: string
   - target_date: if specified

=== WEEKLY AGGREGATION LOGIC ===

WEEKLY ACTIVITY SUMMARY (for "Detail this week" column):
1. Group activities by equipment / area across all daily reports of the week
2. Identify CxL milestones achieved (completed CxL2A/B for which equipment)
3. Collapse repeated monitoring into single summary bullets
4. Keep document submission events as-is (they're milestones)
5. Format under category headers in brackets: [Cx Schedule and Activity], [Document Submission], [Issues & Resolution]

NEXT WEEK PLAN (for "Next Plan" column):
1. Take all NEXT_PLAN items from the last daily report of the week (Friday)
2. Cross-reference with TWLA schedule for week+1 dates
3. Add any carry-over open issues
4. Format as short action bullets

=== OUTPUT FORMAT (matches boss's weekly report) ===
File: E&S Team_주간업무보고_[month]월[week]주차(최보문M).pptx

Slide per PIC (for Vega M / LSEI scope):
- Header: "Global Project Sales Team Weekly Work Report([DD-Mon]~[DD-Mon])"
- Table: PIC | Main Project | Detail(This week) | Next Plan
- Main Project: "JK1-2 Equinix DC"
- Category headers in brackets, ? bullet prefix
- Status inline: (Done), (In Progress), (Pending)
- Remarks: section at bottom with dated entries

=== TWLA EXTRACTION LOGIC ===
- Read xlsx sheet "2WLA Sch"
- Row = date (col A), Columns B-H = Level 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, Roof
- For each date in current week: extract planned activities per level
- Match against daily report equipment codes to determine actual vs planned
- Flag slippage: planned item not found in daily reports = DELAYED

=== EQUIPMENT CODE REGISTRY (from Supply List) ===
LSEI-supplied equipment only:
- 1RMU_B → Level 3
- 1RMU_C → Level 5
- 1RMU_D → Level 5
- 1RMU_F → Level 7
- 1MLTX_B → Level 3
- 1MLTX_C → Level 5
- 1MLTX_D → Level 5
- 1MLTX_F → Level 7
- BTU variants (B/C/D/F) at same levels
- Control Box variants at same levels

=== STEP-BY-STEP GENERATION WORKFLOW ===

When asked to generate weekly report:

STEP 1 — Read all daily reports for the target week
  → Extract structured data (activities, docs, issues, plans)

STEP 2 — Read TWLA for that week range
  → Map planned activities vs date

STEP 3 — Aggregate weekly summary
  → Group by equipment → summarize CxL progress
  → List document milestones
  → Identify key issues (closed + still open)

STEP 4 — Extract next week plan
  → From last day's Next Plan + TWLA week+1

STEP 5 — Generate PPTX in boss's format
  → One slide per PIC, table layout
  → Vega M slide = LSEI/JK1-2 Equinix scope

STEP 6 — Save to: F:\Project\Equinix Project Data Teknis Dari LS\Weekly Report\

=== TRIGGER PHRASES ===
"buat weekly report minggu ini" → generate for current week
"update weekly report" → re-read daily files and regenerate
"summary minggu ini" → text-only version without PPTX

topic: CORE_RULE: Document Generation Tool Priority (Zapier First)
content:
CORE STANDING RULE — Document Generation Tool Priority

For any document creation task, CORE always uses these tools in order of priority:

1. GOOGLE SLIDES (via Zapier) → for any presentation / slide deck task
   - Zapier tools: google_slides_create_presentation_from_template, google_slides_find_and_replace_text
   - If no template exists yet, create one first then fill via Zapier

2. GOOGLE DOCS (via Zapier) → for any document / report / memo / letter task
   - Zapier tools: google_docs_create_document_from_text, google_docs_find_and_replace_text, google_docs_append_text_to_document

3. GOOGLE SHEETS (via Zapier) → for any spreadsheet / tracker / table task
   - Zapier tools: google_sheets_create_spreadsheet, google_sheets_create_spreadsheet_row, google_sheets_update_spreadsheet_row

4. IF task requires a tool/integration NOT yet connected via Zapier:
   → CORE must inform Vux explicitly: "Untuk task ini butuh [tool/service], please connect dulu ke Zapier di: zapier.com/app/connections"
   → Do NOT attempt workaround silently — always ask first

NEVER default to: local PPTX (python-pptx), local DOCX, or local XLSX unless Vux explicitly asks for local file output.

This rule applies to ALL future tasks involving document creation, report generation, or data tabulation.

topic: LSEI_SCOPE: JK1-2 Equinix Supply Boundary

OUR SCOPE (M. Rahmat / LSEI):
- MV Panel (RMU): 1RMU_B, 1RMU_C, 1RMU_D, 1RMU_F
- Transformer (MLTX): 1MLTX_B, 1MLTX_C, 1MLTX_D, 1MLTX_F
- BTU (Battery Termination Unit) — included under RMU/MV scope: 1RMU_B_BTU, 1RMU_C_BTU, 1RMU_D_BTU, 1RMU_F_BTU
- Control Box MV Panel (per RMU location)

NOT OUR SCOPE — EXCLUDE from weekly/daily report:
- DCOS cable laying (bukan supply LSEI)
- Busduct (bukan supply LSEI)
- RPP panels
- CCTV, ACS, VESDA, Dry Sprinkler, CHW Pipe, CRAH, ASD, DB panels
- STSSB, MDSB, MSB, UDP, UPS (semua LV distribution — bukan LSEI)
- RFIT positioning untuk non-LSEI equipment

FILTER RULE for report generation:
- Only include activities directly related to: RMU, MLTX, BTU, Control Box
- Supervision/witness activities for other equipment can be mentioned only if M. Rahmat was explicitly the supervisor/witness
- DCOS, busduct, LV panels → EXCLUDE always

topic: Add KB entry for Python function debugging best practices

topic: CORE GOD MODE — Agentic Tool Improvement Master Plan v1.0
content:
## CORE GOD MODE — Master Tool Improvement Plan
Created: 2026-03-13 | Session: claude-desktop | Status: PENDING EXECUTION

### ROOT CAUSE OF SLOWNESS THIS SESSION
1. core.py is 136KB — GitHub /contents/ API silently fails above ~1MB decoded, but also gets slow/unreliable near that size
2. No way to find functions without binary-search (3-5 gh_read_lines calls per function)
3. Every edit = separate fetch+replace+write cycle
4. No atomic "patch + wait for deploy" — always 2+ separate steps
5. gh_search_replace fetches the whole file every call — 6 patches = 6 full fetches
6. No visibility into live stdout logs
7. Tool indentation bugs: old_str matching fails on whitespace differences
8. claude.ai (web) cannot safely edit core.py at all — no safe path exists from web

---

### CATEGORY 1: FIX CRITICAL BUGS IN EXISTING TOOLS (P5 — DO FIRST)

**1A. gh_search_replace — switch to Git Blobs API**
- Problem: uses /contents/ API which has ~1MB limit and fails silently on large files
- Fix: fetch via GET /repos/{repo}/git/blobs/{sha} with Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.raw
  - Get blob SHA from tree: GET /git/trees/{tree_sha}
  - Write back via: create new blob → new tree → new commit → update ref
- This is the same path PowerShell scripts use and it works perfectly at any file size
- Impact: ALL gh_search_replace calls become reliable regardless of core.py growth

**1B. multi_patch — same fix as 1A**
- Already written but uses /contents/ API internally
- Rewrite to use Git Blobs API — same fix as 1A
- One fetch, N replacements, one atomic write

**1C. t_gh_read_lines — switch to Git Blobs API**
- Same /contents/ size issue
- Already works for now (136KB is still ok for read) but will break as core.py grows
- Preemptive fix

---

### CATEGORY 2: NEW POWER TOOLS (P4)

**2A. t_core_py_edit(patches, message) — DEDICATED core.py editor**
- Hardcoded to core.py, uses Git Blobs API always
- Accepts array of {old_str, new_str} patches
- Auto-fetches latest blob SHA (no stale SHA issues)
- Returns: {ok, applied, skipped, new_line_count, commit_sha}
- Why separate from multi_patch: core.py is special — needs extra validation
  - Syntax check: count def/class/if/try blocks for basic balance
  - Size guard: warn if file grows > 200KB
  - Auto-logs to mistakes if patch fails

**2B. t_core_py_fn(fn_name) — read a single function from core.py**
- Input: function name string e.g. "t_redeploy"  
- Output: exact source of that function, start/end line numbers
- Implementation: search_in_file for "def {fn_name}" → read lines until next "def " at same indent
- Replaces 3-5 gh_read_lines calls per function lookup
- Most common operation this session — used ~20 times

**2C. t_core_py_validate() — pre-deploy syntax check**
- Fetch core.py, run basic checks:
  - All def/class blocks opened and closed
  - No "def def" double keywords (past incident)
  - TOOLS dict has closing }
  - All functions referenced in TOOLS dict actually exist in file
  - No RAILWAY_TOKEN references (regression check)
- Returns: {ok, warnings[], errors[]}
- Run before every deploy automatically

**2D. t_session_start() — one-call session bootstrap**
- Currently start of every session requires: get_state + get_system_health + search_kb(mistakes) + check_evolutions
- Bundle into one tool: runs all 4, returns combined snapshot
- Returns: {health, counts, pending_evolutions, recent_mistakes, step, last_session_summary}
- Saves 3-4 tool calls at start of every single session

**2E. t_session_end(summary, actions, domain, patterns, quality) — one-call session close**
- Currently end of session requires: sb_insert(sessions) + reflect() + update_state + sometimes log_mistake
- Bundle into one tool: runs all in sequence, returns confirmation
- Critical: session data is often lost because closing steps are tedious
- Also auto-updates SESSION.md if summary contains step change keywords

**2F. t_core_py_rollback(commit_sha) — emergency restore**
- Input: commit SHA (short or full)
- Fetches core.py at that specific commit via git API
- Writes it back as new commit on main
- Use case: bad patch deployed, need to revert fast without manual GitHub UI
- Currently this takes 5+ manual steps

**2G. t_sb_bulk_insert(table, rows_json) — insert multiple rows at once**
- Current sb_insert only does 1 row at a time
- Supabase PostgREST supports array inserts natively
- Use case: inserting multiple KB entries, multiple backlog items in one call

**2H. t_diff(path, commit_sha_a, commit_sha_b) — compare two versions**
- Show what changed between two commits for a specific file
- Use case: "what exactly did I change in that last patch?" — currently impossible without GitHub UI
- Implementation: fetch both blobs, compute unified diff

---

### CATEGORY 3: IMPROVE EXISTING TOOLS (P3)

**3A. search_in_file — make case-sensitive option + regex support**
- Add params: case_sensitive=false (default), regex=false
- Regex mode: pattern is treated as Python regex
- Use case: find all "def t_" functions, find all "RAILWAY_TOKEN" occurrences

**3B. gh_search_replace — add dry_run mode**
- dry_run=true: shows what WOULD be replaced without committing
- Prevents wrong replacements — verify first, then apply
- Returns: {would_replace: true, context_before, context_after, line_number}

**3C. deploy_and_wait — add progress notifications via Telegram**
- Currently silent — you don't know it's polling
- If timeout > 60s, send Telegram message when deploy starts + when it completes
- Use case: trigger deploy from Desktop, go do something else, get Telegram ping when done

**3D. build_status — show last 5 commits with status, not just last 3**
- Minor: increase default from 3 to 5, add "time_since" field in human readable format

**3E. get_backlog — add type filter**
- Current: filter by status + min_priority
- Add: filter by type (new_tool, logic_improvement, etc.)
- Use case: "show me all new_tool backlog items" — current requires manual filtering

**3F. logs — add filter by keyword**
- Current: returns last N commit messages
- Add: keyword filter to find specific deploys
- Use case: "find the commit where I fixed t_redeploy"

---

### CATEGORY 4: DELETE / DEPRECATE (P2)

**4A. route tool — DEPRECATE**
- t_route() runs Groq to "route" a task — was for Telegram free-text mode
- Telegram free-text was removed this session
- queue_poller still uses it but that should be simplified too
- Replace with: direct Groq call in the specific context needed

**4B. write_file — ADD core.py GUARD**
- write_file does FULL OVERWRITE — catastrophic if used on core.py
- Add guard: if path == "core.py": return error "Use core_py_edit or gh_search_replace instead"
- This has caused corruption before

**4C. task_queue via Telegram — REMOVE queue_poller auto-execution**
- queue_poller auto-executes pending tasks via t_route — dangerous, runs without owner
- Telegram free-text removed, so nothing should be queuing tasks autonomously
- Simplify: queue_poller should only notify owner of pending tasks, not execute them

---

### CATEGORY 5: ARCHITECTURE IMPROVEMENTS (P2)

**5A. core.py split into modules**
- At 136KB, core.py is hitting GitHub API limits
- Split into: core_main.py (FastAPI + MCP dispatcher), core_tools.py (all t_* functions), core_train.py (training pipeline), core_github.py (Git API helpers)
- Import chain: core_main.py imports from others
- Benefit: each file stays under 50KB, no more API limit issues ever
- This is the single biggest architectural improvement possible

**5B. /patch endpoint — make it the canonical edit path from claude.ai**
- POST /patch already exists and works
- Problem: requires MCP_SECRET which claude.ai doesn't know
- Solution: add it to the MCP startup context so claude.ai sessions can use it
- This gives claude.ai a safe surgical edit path (not full file overwrite)

**5C. Git Blobs API helper — _gh_blob_read(path) and _gh_blob_write(path, content, message)**
- Centralize the blob read/write pattern used by PowerShell scripts
- All gh_* tools use this instead of /contents/ API
- Once implemented in core.py, ALL tools automatically get large-file support

---

### EXECUTION ORDER
1. P5: Fix 1A (gh_search_replace → blobs API) — unblocks everything else
2. P5: Fix 1B (multi_patch → blobs API)
3. P4: Build 2C (core_py_validate) — safety net before more edits
4. P4: Build 2B (core_py_fn) — biggest daily time saver
5. P4: Build 2A (core_py_edit) — dedicated safe editor
6. P4: Build 2D+2E (session_start + session_end) — quality of life
7. P3: Category 3 improvements
8. P2: Architecture split (5A) — biggest long-term fix

---
### STATUS: PENDING — registered 2026-03-13, awaiting execution per category

topic: Windows-MCP: Full Tool Reference & PC Control Capabilities
content:
Windows-MCP (CursorTouch/Windows-MCP) is a lightweight Python MCP server that gives AI agents full control over a Windows PC via the accessibility tree + Win32 APIs. No computer vision needed — works with any LLM.

TOOL INVENTORY (17 tools available in current CORE session):
- Snapshot: Full desktop state — active apps, UI elements (a11y tree), optional screenshot (use_vision=True), optional browser DOM (use_dom=True). Most important tool — always start here.
- Click: Mouse click at [x,y] coordinates or UI element label/id
- Type: Type text at [x,y] or element label (pastes entire block — not ideal for IDE code)
- Move: Move mouse to coordinates or element
- Scroll: Scroll at coordinates, element, or current mouse position
- Drag: Drag from one point to another
- Shortcut: Send keyboard combos (Ctrl+C, Alt+Tab, Win+D, etc.)
- Clipboard: Read or set Windows clipboard content
- MultiEdit: Enter text into multiple input fields at once (batch form fill)
- MultiSelect: Select multiple items with optional Ctrl key (files, checkboxes)
- App: Launch app from start menu, resize/move window, switch between apps
- PowerShell: Execute PowerShell commands — CAUTION: Railway HTTP calls timeout silently here, always use core-agi MCP tools instead
- Process: List running processes or kill by PID/name
- Notification: Send Windows toast notification (title + message)
- Scrape: Fetch full webpage text content from URL
- Wait: Pause execution N seconds
- FileSystem: Read/write files, 8 modes (read, write, list, create, delete, move, copy, info)

KNOWN LIMITATIONS:
- Fine-grained text selection within a paragraph not yet supported (uses a11y tree)
- Type-Tool pastes whole block, not keystroke-by-keystroke — bad for IDE coding
- App-Tool may fail on non-English Windows — disable it or use English
- PowerShell silently times out for network calls — never use for Railway/GitHub HTTP

CORRECT WORKFLOW FOR PC CONTROL:
1. Always call Snapshot first to see current desktop state
2. Identify target element by label/id from snapshot
3. Click/Type/Shortcut as needed
4. Snapshot again to verify result

SETUP (on the PC):
- Requires Python 3.12+ and UV package manager
- Install: git clone https://github.com/CursorTouch/Windows-MCP.git
- Run: uv --directory <path> run windows-mcp
- Config in claude_desktop_config.json (MSIX Claude path differs — use %LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Claude_pzs8sxrjxfjjc\LocalCache\Roaming\Claude\)

NOTE: Ki's PC is an iMac (macOS), not Windows. Windows-MCP tools appear connected via Claude Desktop session routing — likely via a Windows VM or remote setup. Always confirm desktop state with Snapshot before assuming OS.

topic: CORE v6 Training Design — Phases 6-12 & Soul Architecture
content:
CORE v6 TRAINING DESIGN (TRAINING_DESIGN.md — 48KB, to be deleted in Task 1 after absorption):

Two signal tracks, both feed same pipeline:
- TRACK A (Real): reads sessions + mistakes from Supabase → Groq extracts patterns → writes to hot_reflections (source=real)
- TRACK B (Simulation): loads CORE context → Groq simulates 1M users (40% routine, 30% complex, 20% edge cases, 10% failure recovery) → writes to hot_reflections (source=simulation)

Cold Processor confidence weighting:
- source=real only → base confidence
- source=simulation only → base × 0.7
- source=both (overlap) → base × 1.3 (highest signal)

NEVER auto-applies anything. Owner + Claude Desktop are the only hands.

Phases 6-12 planned (AGI self-management):
- Phase 6: Railway AGI Tools (t_deploy_status, t_crash_report, t_rollback, t_incident_detect, t_self_upgrade_pipeline — CORE manages own deployment)
- Phase 7: Supabase AGI Tools (t_schema_diff, t_migrate, t_compact_kb, t_snapshot, t_self_heal_pipeline — CORE manages own DB)
- Phase 8: GitHub AGI Tools (t_patch_validate, t_anchor_check, t_function_list, t_tools_audit, t_evolution_patch — CORE manages own codebase)
- Phase 9: Telegram AGI Tools (t_tg_send_markdown, t_tg_inline_keyboard, t_tg_daily_brief, t_tg_voice_note — CORE owns comms layer)
- Phase 10: The Soul — provider abstraction (t_soul_think wraps all groq_chat calls, t_soul_fallback, t_soul_with_kb, t_soul_fine_tune_prep, t_soul_graduation — CORE eventually trains its own model)
- Phase 11: Claude Desktop AGI — core_agent.py as persistent background agent (Windows Task Scheduler every 5min, t_cd_autonomous_loop, t_cd_decision_gate classifies risk before any action)
- Phase 12: Full autonomous loop (CORE self-improves while PC is on)

core_agent.py: Already built at C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\projects\ (or nearby). Bridges PC to CORE Railway.

topic: Autonomous self-chat loop protocol
content:
AUTONOMOUS MODE PROTOCOL — Claude Desktop self-chat loop.

ACTIVATION: When user says "activate autonomous mode", Claude must:
1. Launch daemon: Desktop Commander:start_process → C:\Python314\python.exe "C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\selfchat\core_selfchat.py" --mode watch
2. Write seed prompt to C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\selfchat\prompt.txt
3. Daemon sends prompt when Claude is idle (polls for absence of "Stop response" button)
4. Claude responds → IMMEDIATELY writes next prompt to prompt.txt to continue loop
5. Repeat until task done or user says stop

CRITICAL RULE: Claude must write next prompt to prompt.txt AT THE END OF EVERY RESPONSE during autonomous mode. If Claude forgets this, the loop dies after turn 1.

STOP: Write "stop" to C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\selfchat\status.txt

FILES:
  prompt.txt   → Claude writes next prompt here after each response
  response.txt → optional, Claude can write summary of its response here
  status.txt   → idle/running/stop
  log.jsonl    → conversation history

MECHANISM: Clipboard → Click (983,937) → Ctrl+V → Return (only when idle)
PYTHON: C:\Python314\python.exe
DAEMON SCRIPT: C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\selfchat\core_selfchat.py

topic: Claude Desktop idle detection and window focus
content: Claude Desktop self-chat: IDLE DETECTION works via PowerShell UIAutomation — checks for absence of "Stop response" button. Returns True=idle, False=busy. Test: C:\Python314\python.exe core_selfchat.py --test-idle. AppActivate focus method: (New-Object -ComObject WScript.Shell).AppActivate('Claude') — safe, does not minimize or open wrong windows. Old SetForegroundWindow method caused Claude to minimize and open Recycle Bin — do not use.

topic: MISTAKE: autonomous loop dies after turn 1 — must write prompt.txt
content: MISTAKE — Autonomous loop dies after turn 1. Root cause: Claude responds but never writes next prompt to prompt.txt. Daemon watches prompt.txt for changes — if Claude doesn't update it, loop stops. Fix: always end every autonomous response by writing next prompt to prompt.txt via windows-mcp:FileSystem write.

topic: Claude Desktop no auto-scroll — must scroll to bottom before each send
content: Claude Desktop does NOT auto-scroll. During autonomous self-chat loop, before every send the daemon must: 1) click chat body area (744, 500), 2) press End key, 3) click scroll-to-bottom button (~979, 867 when visible). Without this, the input box coords may be wrong and prompts go to the wrong place. scroll_to_bottom() function handles this in core_selfchat.py — always call it inside send_prompt() before pasting.

topic: Claude Desktop scroll to bottom — mouse wheel only method
content: Claude Desktop scroll method: End key does NOT work. 'Scroll to bottom' button click only works when Claude is IDLE (locked during streaming). WORKING METHOD: mouse wheel scroll on chat body area (744, 500). Via windows-mcp: Scroll(direction=down, loc=[744,500], wheel_times=20). Via pyautogui in daemon: pyautogui.moveTo(744,500); pyautogui.scroll(-20). Use wheel_times=20 or more for long chats. Always call AFTER wait_for_idle(), not during streaming.

  (each task ~8-12% context depending on file reads)

  because /patch calls from claude.ai are possible but costly
- Safest task types for claude.ai autonomous: KB hygiene, cold processor runs, 
  doc writes (write_file to docs/), SESSION.md updates, knowledge synthesis
- Never start Task 2 (architecture split) from claude.ai — requires core.py multi_patch 
  which is risky without Desktop Commander read-back
- Always end with session_end — same as Desktop sessions

topic: Read-back confirmation rule — never say done without verifying
content:
CORE MISTAKE PATTERN — Read-back confirmation rule (2026-03-13):

One of the most recurring failure modes across 97 logged mistakes: claiming success without read-back.

RULE: Never say done/confirmed/pushed/stored until a read-back operation confirms expected state.
Format: "[VERIFY OK] — {what was confirmed}" 

Applied to each operation type:
- write_file → immediately call read_file on same path, confirm last ~5 lines match
- sb_insert → call sb_query on same table with filters to confirm row exists
- gh_search_replace → call gh_read_lines on modified section to verify change
- redeploy → call get_system_health or /state after ~30s to confirm Railway is live
- session_end → call session_start in next session, verify last_session matches summary

This applies in ALL modes: Claude Desktop, claude.ai, autonomous, manual.
Root cause of violations: optimistic trust in tool response "ok:true" without verifying state externally.

topic: Cold processor — trigger conditions and expected behavior
content:
COLD PROCESSOR — Trigger conditions and expected output (2026-03-13):

When to run: 
- After 5+ hot_reflections have accumulated (currently: 0 unprocessed)
- After a heavy mistake-logging session (10+ mistakes in one domain)
- Before a major code change (Task 2, Task 3, etc.) to freeze learnings
- At end of a multi-session sprint (e.g., after repo cleanup sprint)

What it does:
- Reads hot_reflections (source=real and source=simulation)
- Extracts patterns via Groq
- Writes to cold_reflections with confidence weighting:
  source=real only → base confidence
  source=simulation only → base × 0.7  
  source=both (overlap) → base × 1.3 (highest signal)
- Queues patterns as evolution_queue items for owner review

Current state (2026-03-13): hot_reflections = 0, evolution_queue = 0.
Cold processor will find nothing to process if hot_reflections = 0.
Must log hot_reflections first (via reflect() tool) before trigger_cold_processor is useful.

PATTERN: Each session should call reflect() at end → cold processor runs on next trigger cycle.

topic: CORE AGI Skill File — Location & Architecture-Agnostic Rules
content:
The CORE AGI skill file for claude.ai is located at: C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\CORE_AGI_SKILL.md

CRITICAL RULE — Architecture Is Live Data:
Never hardcode source file names (e.g. core.py, core_main.py, any module name) in the skill file or in session responses. CORE's architecture changes across versions. Always fetch current entry point and active files from:
  session_start → operating_context.architecture.entry_point

The skill file has been updated (2026-03-14) to be fully architecture-agnostic:
- Removed all hardcoded file name references
- Added explicit rule: if about to type a specific filename, stop and fetch live state first
- Editing principles are now interface-based (Desktop vs claude.ai), not file-name-based
- From claude.ai: always use POST /patch for source edits
- From Claude Desktop: use gh_search_replace (small) or github:push_files (full restore)

This KB entry should be checked at the start of any session involving skill file updates or source code edits.

topic: Session start — mandatory interface detection SOP
content:
MANDATORY at every session start, no exceptions:

1. Run tool_search("core-agi session_start") — if session_start responds = Claude Desktop
2. If no core-agi tools = claude.ai (Desktop Commander available but no CORE MCP)
3. If no Desktop Commander either = mobile (most restricted)
4. Never assume interface from previous session context or conversation history
5. Log correct interface in sessions table: claude-desktop / claude.ai / mobile

CAPABILITY MATRIX:
- Claude Desktop: all 50 core-agi MCP tools, Desktop Commander, Windows-MCP, full agentic ops
- claude.ai: github: tools, zapier: tools, web_fetch — NO core-agi MCP, NO PowerShell for Railway/GitHub
- Mobile: web_fetch only — no file ops, no MCP tools

SINGLE tool_search query loads all session tools in 1 call — never search per-tool individually.

SOURCE: pattern_frequency id=28, frequency=190 (highest in system). Caused multiple sessions of wasted PowerShell workarounds when on claude.ai assuming Desktop.

topic: Write-then-read verification SOP — every remote write requires readback
content:
RULE: Every remote write must be followed by a read-back confirming expected state. Only declare success after read confirms.

PROTOCOL:
1. Execute write (sb_insert, gh_search_replace, write_file, etc.)
2. Immediately read back the affected resource
3. Confirm expected state matches actual state
4. Only then output [VERIFY OK] + what was confirmed
5. If mismatch: output [VERIFY FAIL] + what was wrong + retry or escalate

NEVER say: "done", "confirmed", "pushed", "stored", "updated" based on API response alone.
APIs return ok:true / 201 / 200 even when operation partially failed, was rate-limited, or had wrong content.

APPLIES TO:
- All sb_insert / sb_bulk_insert → read back with sb_query
- All GitHub writes → read back with get_file_contents or gh_read_lines
- All SESSION.md patches → fetch raw URL to confirm
- All Railway deploys → deploy_status + verify_live

SOURCE: mistakes #79 (critical), #80 (critical), #81 (critical). verify_rate across historical sessions averages 0.05-0.12 — this is the most violated rule in CORE history.

topic: Bulk destructive operations — read + summarize + confirm SOP
content:
RULE: Any bulk destructive operation (delete >10 files or >10 DB rows) requires a 3-step gate before executing:

STEP 1 — READ: Read/list every item to be deleted. No exceptions.
STEP 2 — SUMMARIZE: Output a summary of what each item is and whether it is safe to delete. Register any useful items (scripts, docs, KB entries) before removing them.
STEP 3 — CONFIRM: Notify owner via Telegram or explicit message. Wait for confirmation before proceeding.

NEVER use:
- Remove-Item -Force on unknown files (bypasses Recycle Bin — permanent)
- Supabase bulk DELETE without reading contents first
- GitHub file deletion without checking if content is already committed elsewhere

SOURCE: mistake #179 — 263 scripts permanently deleted without audit. Only 18 of ~280 were registered. No recovery possible. Remove-Item -Force bypassed Recycle Bin.

topic: railway.json startCommand overrides Procfile — check order for Railway entry point
content:
railway.json startCommand silently overrides Procfile. This caused v6.0 activation to fail — Procfile was updated to core_main.py but railway.json still pointed to core.py monolith. Railway used railway.json, ignored Procfile entirely.

RULE: Whenever a Procfile change has no visible effect on Railway deploy, check railway.json startCommand immediately — it takes precedence.

CHECK ORDER:
1. railway.json startCommand (highest priority)
2. Procfile web: directive
3. Framework auto-detection (lowest priority)

FREQUENCY: Hit 3x across real sessions (pattern_frequency id=207, freq=3). Queued as evolution after third occurrence.

topic: Module extraction from monolith — always read full source first
content:
Before extracting any module from a monolith (core.py → core_tools.py etc.), always read the FULL source file first.

WHY: Monoliths accumulate shared globals, helper functions, constants, and cross-references that are invisible from partial reads. Missing one causes import errors, circular dependencies, or silent runtime failures after split.

CHECKLIST before extraction:
1. Read full source (use gh_read_lines in chunks if needed — Blobs API has no size limit)
2. Map all globals and constants used by target module
3. Map all cross-module function calls
4. Check for circular import risks — resolve with lazy imports inside functions
5. Verify import chain is acyclic before committing

FREQUENCY: Hit 4x across sessions (pattern_frequency id=213, freq=4). Core insight from Task 2 architecture split (3 desktop sessions).

topic: bulk-delete-requires-read-summarize-confirm
content: Bulk destructive operations (delete >10 files/rows) require read+summary+owner confirm before executing. Mistake #179: 263 scripts permanently deleted without audit. Rule exists in mistakes but not in KB as SOP.

topic: session-start-interface-detection-freq190-never-in-kb
content: Highest-frequency pattern (190 hits, pattern_frequency id=28): never assume interface — always verify tool availability at session start. Auto-applied at confidence=0.5 but never promoted to KB as proven entry with full SOP.

topic: write-then-read-verify-protocol-missing-from-kb
content: Write-then-read verify protocol (mistakes #79, #80, #81 — all critical). CORE violates this constantly: verify_rate in hot_reflections averages 0.0–0.12. Rule exists in 3 separate critical mistakes but never consolidated as a single KB SOP.

topic: OpenClaw — full integration overview for CORE sessions
content:
OpenClaw is a Gemini-powered AI gateway running natively on Windows (DESKTOP-QBJ5CUH).
Version: v2026.2.26 — installed via npm global at C:\Users\rnvgg\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw

ARCHITECTURE:
- Gateway: ws://127.0.0.1:18789 (local WebSocket server)
- Config: C:\Users\rnvgg\.openclaw\openclaw.json
- Start: node openclaw.mjs gateway
- Auto-start: Windows Task Scheduler task "OpenClawGateway" via start-gateway.ps1
- TUI connect: openclaw tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 --password yourpassword

MODELS:
- Primary: Gemini (free tier, 11 API keys rotating automatically)
- Keys stored: C:\Users\rnvgg\.claude-skills\services\geminiapikey.txt
- Rotation: set GEMINI_API_KEYS as comma-separated env var — OpenClaw rotates on rate limit

CORE INTEGRATION:
- OpenClaw (Gemini) and CORE/Jarvis (Claude) share the same Supabase brain
- OpenClaw uses jarvis-trainer skill: C:\Users\rnvgg\.openclaw\workspace\skills\jarvis-trainer\SKILL.md
- Joint training trigger: "train jarvis", "joint train", or auto on session end
- Both AIs write to same knowledge_base, mistakes, sessions tables

PC INTEGRATION (CORE → OpenClaw):
- CORE can send commands to OpenClaw gateway via WebSocket from Desktop sessions
- Auth token: auto-generated in gateway.auth.token field of openclaw.json on first start
- HTTP health check: GET ws://127.0.0.1:18789 returns 200 when gateway is live
- Password auth required for TUI and API connections

KEY CONFIG RULES:
- gateway.mode must be set to "local" in openclaw.json — otherwise blocks with error
- Old config schema: agent.model → new schema: agents.defaults.model.primary (auto-migrates)
- GEMINI_API_KEY = primary key, GEMINI_API_KEYS = rotation list (both as persistent user env vars)

CHANNELS AVAILABLE: LINE (@openclaw/line), Zalo (@openclaw/zalo), Zalo Personal (@openclaw/zalouser)
SECURITY: exec-approvals.json controls host command permissions. Security modes: deny / allowlist / full

topic: OpenClaw + CORE PC integration — joint training and cross-model workflow
content:
How CORE (Claude) and OpenClaw (Gemini) work together on the same machine:

SHARED BRAIN:
Both AIs write to the same Supabase instance (qbfaplqiakwjvrtwpbmr).
- CORE writes: knowledge_base, mistakes, sessions, hot_reflections via core-agi MCP tools
- OpenClaw writes: knowledge_base, mistakes via jarvis-trainer SKILL.md → POST to brain API
- Result: Claude and Gemini accumulate shared long-term memory across all sessions

JARVIS-TRAINER SKILL (OpenClaw side):
Path: C:\Users\rnvgg\.openclaw\workspace\skills\jarvis-trainer\SKILL.md
Trigger phrases: "train jarvis", "joint train", "save to jarvis"
Action: POSTs learnings to https://jarvis-os-production.up.railway.app brain API

CORE SESSION → OPENCLAW:
From Claude Desktop, CORE can interact with OpenClaw gateway via:
1. Windows-MCP:PowerShell to send WebSocket messages to ws://127.0.0.1:18789
2. Auth: use gateway.auth.token from C:\Users\rnvgg\.openclaw\openclaw.json
3. Verify gateway live: HTTP GET :18789 returns 200

OPENCLAW SESSION → CORE:
OpenClaw sessions can read CORE KB via search_kb MCP tool (if connected)
Or via jarvis-trainer skill POSTing to /brain/search endpoint directly

PRACTICAL WORKFLOW:
- Use CORE (Claude Desktop) for: code edits, Railway deploys, Supabase ops, agentic tasks
- Use OpenClaw (Gemini) for: fast KB lookups, channel responses (LINE/Zalo), lightweight tasks
- Cross-train: end every OpenClaw session with "joint train" to sync learnings to shared KB
- Cross-train: end every CORE Desktop session with session_end (auto-logs to same Supabase)

NOTE: jarvis-os-production.up.railway.app is a separate Railway deployment (Jarvis OS system) — distinct from core-agi-production.up.railway.app (CORE AGI system). Both share the same Supabase DB.

topic: session_end must capture created_at before sb_post and pass to auto_hot_reflection
content: session_end was not passing created_at to auto_hot_reflection, causing session_ts to always fall back to now-2h. Fix: capture session_created_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat() before the sb_post sessions insert, then pass as created_at key into auto_hot_reflection dict. This ensures the 4-table enrichment queries (mistakes, knowledge_base, task_queue, changelog) are scoped to the actual session window.

topic: _extract_real_signal uses last_real_signal_ts state key not hardcoded window
content: _extract_real_signal previously used a hardcoded 24h window. Fixed to use last_real_signal_ts state key stored in sessions table as [state_update] entry. Logic: read last_real_signal_ts → use as since_ts → query sessions+mistakes since that ts → after successful run, write new [state_update] last_real_signal_ts = now. Soft-boot fallback: if no state key found, use yesterday (timedelta(days=1)) so first run after deploy always rescans recent data without starting from epoch.

topic: session_end tool description has mandatory pre-checklist to fill 4 tables before closing
content: session_end tool description now includes a mandatory pre-checklist that Claude must complete before calling session_end: (1) log_mistake for every error, (2) add_knowledge for every new insight, (3) changelog_add for every system change, (4) update task_queue statuses. Without this checklist the 4-table enrichment in auto_hot_reflection would find empty tables and Groq would get a thin narrative with no real signal.

topic: synthesize_evolutions — Claude as CORE unconstrained architect tool
content: New tool t_synthesize_evolutions() planned for Task 8. Fetches all pending evolution_queue + pattern_frequency + cold_reflections + hot_reflection gaps + SESSION.md. Claude (not Groq) reasons across all signals with zero constraints — inventing new tools, tables, architecture changes, logic changes, or wild ideas CORE does not know it needs. Output: structured blueprint with impact/effort matrix per item, appended to SESSION.md as new task chain. Processed evolutions marked status=synthesized. Manual trigger only by owner. Key design principle: Groq extracts isolated patterns, Claude synthesizes the whole picture and invents what is unthinkable from individual signals alone.

topic: t_synthesize_evolutions — implementation complete, 51st MCP tool
content: t_synthesize_evolutions is now live in core_tools.py. It fetches: all pending evolution_queue entries, top 30 pattern_frequency, last 10 cold_reflections, last 20 hot_reflection gaps with gaps_identified, and current SESSION.md. Marks all fetched evolutions as status=synthesized. Returns a structured context payload with architect-level prompt telling Claude to think unconstrained — invent new tools, tables, architecture, logic changes, wild ideas — 6 months ahead. Output format: structured blueprint with category/impact/effort per item, ready to append to SESSION.md as new task chain. Manual trigger only.

topic: Adding new MCP tools must be atomic — function body and TOOLS registry in single push
content: When adding a new MCP tool to core_tools.py, the function body and TOOLS registry entry must be written in a single atomic operation. Never use two separate patches — if the second patch fails or times out, the TOOLS dict will reference a function that doesn't exist yet, causing NameError on import and Railway crash loop. Use github:create_or_update_file or github:push_files to push the complete file atomically. This is especially critical for core_tools.py since it's imported at startup.

topic: File deletion must be atomic — remove file AND all code references in same task
content: When deleting a file from the GitHub repo, always search ALL source files for gh_write and gh_read references to that filename and remove them in the same task. Leaving write references to deleted files causes ghost commits that trigger Railway redeploys mid-session, interrupting active tool calls. Pattern: file deletion must be atomic — delete the file AND remove all code references in one session.

topic: gh_search_replace and multi_patch reliability fix — use gh_read/gh_write not blob API
content: _gh_blob_read/_gh_blob_write use 9-10 sequential GitHub API calls per operation (ref → commit → tree → blob read, then blob create → tree → commit → ref update). High failure rate under Railway MCP timeout. Fixed by swapping both t_gh_search_replace and t_multi_patch to use gh_read/gh_write (Contents API) — just 2 HTTP calls total, proven stable. Note: gh_write returns bool not SHA, so do not try to slice the return value.

topic: synthesize_evolutions — correct usage pattern
content: synthesize_evolutions is a context-delivery tool, not an autonomous generator. Correct workflow: (1) call synthesize_evolutions — it gathers all signals (patterns, cold reflections, gaps, SESSION.md) and returns the full context payload; (2) Claude reads the payload and acts as unconstrained architect, producing the engineering blueprint manually; (3) Claude writes the blueprint to SESSION.md as a new TASK section. The tool does NOT call Groq/Claude internally — Claude IS the architect. multi_patch fails on strings containing unicode box-drawing characters (└, ├, etc) — use github:create_or_update_file for full rewrites when that is the case.

topic: Tool priority system — core-agi vs zapier vs fallback tools
content: Tool priority for CORE sessions: (1) core-agi:* for anything touching CORE's internal state — Supabase, GitHub core-agi repo, Railway, training pipeline, logging. (2) zapier:* for anything reaching external apps — Gmail, Calendar, Todoist, Drive, Gemini, Webhooks, GitHub on non-core-agi repos. (3) github:*, Filesystem:*, Desktop Commander:* as fallbacks for complex GitHub ops or local PC file operations. Gemini (zapier:google_ai_studio_gemini_*) = multimodal, long docs, Google Search grounding, second LLM opinion. Groq (core-agi:ask) = fast, cheap, internal reasoning. Zapier Gemini suite has 8 tools: send_prompt, conversation, generate_image, generate_audio, generate_video, understand_audio, understand_video, understand_youtube_video, understand_document.

topic: core_py_validate — known false positives to ignore
content: core_py_validate triple-quote odd count warning is a false positive — the validator's own source code contains content.count('\"\"\"') on line 482, which the scanner counts as a real triple-quote marker. The odd count is always +1 from this self-referential scan. Ignore this warning unless actual syntax errors appear. Similarly, lines 476-477 errors (stale backboard.railway reference) are the validator's own check strings flagging themselves. Both are pre-existing and harmless.

topic: Project Mode — architecture, tools, and usage patterns
content: Project Mode architecture: projects table (registry) + project_context table (Telegram→Desktop bridge) in Supabase. 9 MCP tools: project_list, project_get, project_search, project_context_check (read); project_register, project_update_kb, project_update_index, project_prepare, project_consume (write). KB entries for projects use domain=project:{project_id}. project_context table bridges Telegram (prepare) → Claude Desktop (get+consume). Railway cannot read local files — only Claude Desktop can index local project folders. After project_get, always call project_consume to mark context clean. /project Telegram command: /project list shows all, /project {id} prepares context.

topic: Project Overview — Equinix JK1-2 DC
content: Equinix JK1-2 DC is a data center project in Jakarta, Indonesia. LSEI (LS Electric Indonesia) is the equipment supplier/contractor. Scope covers two main streams: (1) RMU + BTU — MV switchgear (Ring Main Unit) and Bus Transfer Unit; (2) Transformer — MLTX (Medium-to-Low Voltage Transformer) / CRT (Cast Resin Transformer). Project is currently in active commissioning phase. Reference project: JK1-1 Equinix DC (completed). Folder root: F:\Project\Equinix Project Data Teknis Dari LS\JK1-2 Equinix DC

topic: Document Map — Folder Structure
content: Folder structure for JK1-2 Equinix DC: Two main equipment folders — RMU+BTU and Transformer. Each contains: Design references, DCOS Point List, Calc & Report, Material Specs, Shop Drawing, SOO (Sequence of Operations), Work Method, PTW Template, Schedule Plan. Additional folders: (1) JK1-2 Equinix DC CLX - Comments by Customer, (2) JK1-2 Equinix DC Cx Reports, (3) JK1-2 Equinix DC MLTX- Comments by Customer, (4) Daily Report/referensi (contains Equipment Supply List XLSX), (5) JK1-1 Equinix DC [REFERENCE] (completed reference project with IST handover, MV protection settings, training materials). Key files: JK1-2_Equinix-Weekly_Report_Update-29042025.xlsx (latest schedule), EES-6.1d Equinix LV Switchgear Metering R9 220601.pptx (DCOS reference), Detail Packing List MV Switchgear.pdf, Detail Packing List CRT.pdf.

topic: Phase 2 Deep Read — Priority Documents
content: Phase 2 deep read priority for Equinix JK1-2: (1) JK1-2 Equinix DC CLX - Comments by Customer — customer comment resolution status for CLX stream; (2) JK1-2 Equinix DC MLTX- Comments by Customer — transformer/MLTX customer comments; (3) JK1-2 Equinix DC Cx Reports — commissioning test results and pass/fail status; (4) SOO (Sequence Of Operations) RMU — operating sequences for MV switchgear; (5) SOO (Sequence Of Operations) MLTX — transformer operating sequences; (6) JK1-2_Equinix-Weekly_Report_Update-29042025.xlsx — latest schedule and progress. Each document: read → extract facts → project_update_kb → project_update_index.

topic: CORE File Edit — Unicode Safety Rule
content: gh_search_replace and multi_patch silently fail when old_str contains non-ASCII characters (em-dashes U+2014, curly quotes, etc). Root cause: JSON encoding sends \u2014 but Python string matching may not align depending on how the payload is deserialized. As of Task 10.A (2026-03-14), both tools now include a Unicode pre-flight check: if old_str contains any char with ord > 127, the tool immediately returns ok=false with error="unicode_in_old_str" and a hint to use github:get_file_contents + github:create_or_update_file instead. Rule: when you see this error, do NOT retry gh_search_replace. Fetch the full file, edit in memory, push the corrected version with the SHA. This bypasses encoding entirely and is guaranteed to work.

content:
File: RAMS\RAMS MLTX.docx
Project: equinix-jk1-2
Type: .docx
Size: 18 KB
---
RAMS (Risk Assessment Method Statement)
Risk Rating:
1–5 = Low Risk
6–10 = Medium Risk
11–15 = High Risk
No | Activity / Task | Potential Hazard | Possible Consequence | Likelihood (P) | Severity (S) | Risk Level (R = P × S) | Existing Control Measures | Additional Recommended Controls
1 | Transporting transformer to site | Load drop, forklift collision | Serious injury, equipment damage | 3 | 4 | 12 (High) | Certified operator, clear route | Assign spotter and safety signage
2 | Lifting / rigging | Sling or shackle failure | Fatal injury, transformer damage | 3 | 5 | 15 (High) | Proper rigging and inspection | Direct HSE supervision
3 | Positioning on plinth | Finger pinch, unstable load | Moderate injury | 2 | 3 | 6 (Medium) | Use guide ropes, maintain safe distance | Add foot protection barriers
4 | HV / LV cable connection | Electrical contact / induced voltage | Electric shock, burns | 3 | 5 | 15 (High) | De-energized system, insulated gloves | Implement Lockout/Tagout (LOTO)
5 | Insulation / hipot testing | High test voltage | Electric shock | 2 | 5 | 10 (High) | Isolate area, warning signs | Apply discharge grounding after test
6 | Commissioning (energizing) | Wrong wiring, overcurrent | Short circuit, arc flash | 2 | 5 | 10 (High) | Verify connection and protection devices | Add visual cross-check by engineer
7 | Routine maintenance | Dust / humidity accumulation | Reduced insulation resistance | 3 | 2 | 6 (Medium) | Regular cleaning and inspection | Add temperature and humidity monitoring
8 | Overheating during operation | Poor ventilation | Fire, transformer failure | 2 | 4 | 8 (Medium) | Adequate ventilation provided | Install automatic temperature sensors
Likelihood (P) | Description
1 | Rare – very unlikely to occur
2 | Occasional – may occur under certain conditions
3 | Frequent – likely to occur during normal operation
Severity (S) | Description
1 | Minor – first aid only
2 | Moderate – medical treatment required
3 | Major – lost time injury
4 | Sever

topic: equinix-jk1-2 | RMU + BTU > 00 Design references > 06. DCOS Point List > EBCS-1 Data Center Operations System Standards > EBCS-1 Building Management System Standards.pdf
content:
File: RMU + BTU\00 Design references\06. DCOS Point List\EBCS-1 Data Center Operations System Standards\EBCS-1 Building Management System Standards.pdf

---
Building Management System 
Page 1 of 10 
Version: 1.5 
 
Global Design Standards 
Equinix Confidential 
Date: 2020-07-02 
GLOBAL IBX STANDARD: 
EBCS-1: Data Center Operations System 
 
 
Content Approval: Craig Pennington, Rick Matteazzi, Diraj Bamola 
Distribution: Ops Engineering, Design Engineering, Product Management, Site Ops, Construction  
 
 
History: 
 
Application of Standards 
Standards Documents contain requirements that are to be complied with on a global basis. However, it is 
recognized that market, operational and technical requirements will change over time, so input from regional 
engineering teams and other stakeholders is encouraged. Also, as regional teams work with outside engineering 
firms, it should be emphasized that those firms are responsible to provide designs and detailed construction 
documents that are fully compliant with all applicable codes, and that conflicts between Equinix standards and 
applicable codes are to be brought to our attention. 
 
Purpose 
The Data Center Operations System (DCOS) shall be a layered and networked architecture of controllers and 
devices with functionality for continuous operation and monitoring of building mechanical and electrical systems. 
In this document DCOS refers to all local mechanical and electrical control and monitoring systems which may 
also locally be called Building Management Systems (BMS), Energy Management Systems (EMS), Electrical 
Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) or optimization systems.  
 
This design standard shall be used as the basis of design requirements to aid the engineer in BMS system design 
for International Business Exchange (IBX). 
 
Applicability 
This standard establishes DCOS system architecture and functional criteria for all Greenfield sites as well as site 
expansions or other projects with a total project cost >USD15M. Site expansions or other projects at existing sites 
where the total project cost is <USD15M m

Branch Circuit Monitoring 
Page 1 of 3 
Version: 0.75 
 
Global Design Standards 
Equinix Confidential 
Date:  Apr. 2, 2015 
 
GLOBAL IBX STANDARD: 
EES-10:  Branch Circuit Monitoring  
 
 
Content Approval: Rick Matteazzi 
Distribution: Ops Engineering, Design Engineering, Product Management, Site Ops, Construction  
Contributors: Ernest Holloway, Paul Crouch, Rajesh Byre Gowda, Derrek Telkamp 
 
History: 
 
Application of Standards 
Standards Documents contain requirements that are to be complied with on a global basis.  However, it 
is recognized that market, operational and technical requirements will change over time, so input from 
regional engineering teams and other stakeholders is encouraged.  Also, as regional teams work with 
outside engineering firms, it should be emphasized that those firms are responsible to provide designs 
and detailed construction documents that are fully compliant with all applicable codes, and that conflicts 
between Equinix standards and applicable codes are to be brought to our attention. 
 
Purpose 
This document defines the hardware selection to be used in Branch Circuit Monitoring systems (BCM) 
at all Equinix IBXs. 
 
Applicability 
This document applies to all Equinix IBX facilities and is to be used as reference for design elements of 
BCM and their selection and implementation.  It is supplementary to other applicable standards 
including, but not limited to, topology,  and global procurement. 
 
1.0   RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Global Operations Engineering shall maintain this standard with the assistance of the BCM stake 
holders. 
It is the responsibility of all Operations Engineering, Design Engineering, and Construction personnel to 
comply with this policy.   
It is the responsibility of the Manager Facilities Operations and/or Assistant Manager Facilities 
Operations to ensure that all Site Operations Engineering personnel are aware, understand, and have 
been trained on the implementation of this policy and its requirem

content:
File: RMU + BTU\00 Design references\06. DCOS Point List\EGS-2 Equipment Naming Standards\EGS-2 Global Naming Standard.pdf
Project: equinix-jk1-2
Type: .pdf
Size: 370 KB
---
EGS-2 Global Naming Standard 
Page 1 of 11 
Version: 0.09 
 
Global Design Standards 
Equinix Confidential 
Date: 2021-11-17 
GLOBAL IBX STANDARD: 
EGS-2: Equipment and DCOS Naming 
 
 
Content Approval:  
Distribution: Ops Engineering, Design Engineering, Product Management, Site Ops, Construction  
 
 
History: 
 
Application of Standards 
Standards Documents contain requirements that are to be complied with on a global basis. However, it is 
recognized that market, operational and technical requirements will change over time, so input from regional 
engineering teams and other stakeholders is encouraged. Also, as regional teams work with outside engineering 
firms, it should be emphasized that those firms are responsible to provide designs and detailed construction 
documents that are fully compliant with all applicable codes, and that conflicts between Equinix standards and 
applicable codes are to be brought to our attention. 
 
Purpose 
This document defines equipment, instrument, and tag naming conventions. The equipment name is used on 
design and construction documents, on equipment labels and in the DCOS system. Instrument and tag names 
are used on P&ID drawings, location drawings, instrument and wiring labels, instrument data sheets, equipment 
labels, equipment data sheets, instrument indexes, equipment indexes, field drawings, vendor drawings, and 
equipment/system sequence of operations. This documents also defines tag rules for PLC or other control 
devices. 
 
This document is not intended to be fully prescriptive for the tagging of internal PLC or controller points. PLC 
internal, program scope, variables such as a timer preset do not need to strictly follow these requirements unless 
the point is to be displayed at the DCOS, in which case it should conform to this standard as closely as possible. 
 
This design standard shall be used in conjunction with Standards EBCS-1 and EBCS-3 as the basis of design 
requirements to aid the engineer in system

topic: equinix-jk1-2 | Transformer > 00 Design references MLTX > 06. DCOS Point List > EBCS-1 Data Center Operations System Standards > EBCS-1 Building Management System Standards.pdf
content:

Project: equinix-jk1-2

---
Building Management System 
Page 1 of 10 
Version: 1.5 
 
Global Design Standards 
Equinix Confidential 
Date: 2020-07-02 
GLOBAL IBX STANDARD: 
EBCS-1: Data Center Operations System 
 
 
Content Approval: Craig Pennington, Rick Matteazzi, Diraj Bamola 
Distribution: Ops Engineering, Design Engineering, Product Management, Site Ops, Construction  
 
 
History: 
 
Application of Standards 
Standards Documents contain requirements that are to be complied with on a global basis. However, it is 
recognized that market, operational and technical requirements will change over time, so input from regional 
engineering teams and other stakeholders is encouraged. Also, as regional teams work with outside engineering 
firms, it should be emphasized that those firms are responsible to provide designs and detailed construction 
documents that are fully compliant with all applicable codes, and that conflicts between Equinix standards and 
applicable codes are to be brought to our attention. 
 
Purpose 
The Data Center Operations System (DCOS) shall be a layered and networked architecture of controllers and 
devices with functionality for continuous operation and monitoring of building mechanical and electrical systems. 
In this document DCOS refers to all local mechanical and electrical control and monitoring systems which may 
also locally be called Building Management Systems (BMS), Energy Management Systems (EMS), Electrical 
Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) or optimization systems.  
 
This design standard shall be used as the basis of design requirements to aid the engineer in BMS system design 
for International Business Exchange (IBX). 
 
Applicability 
This standard establishes DCOS system architecture and functional criteria for all Greenfield sites as well as site 
expansions or other projects with a total project cost >USD15M. Site expansions or other projects at existing sites 
where the total project cost is <USD15M m

Branch Circuit Monitoring 
Page 1 of 3 
Version: 0.75 
 
Global Design Standards 
Equinix Confidential 
Date:  Apr. 2, 2015 
 
GLOBAL IBX STANDARD: 
EES-10:  Branch Circuit Monitoring  
 
 
Content Approval: Rick Matteazzi 
Distribution: Ops Engineering, Design Engineering, Product Management, Site Ops, Construction  
Contributors: Ernest Holloway, Paul Crouch, Rajesh Byre Gowda, Derrek Telkamp 
 
History: 
 
Application of Standards 
Standards Documents contain requirements that are to be complied with on a global basis.  However, it 
is recognized that market, operational and technical requirements will change over time, so input from 
regional engineering teams and other stakeholders is encouraged.  Also, as regional teams work with 
outside engineering firms, it should be emphasized that those firms are responsible to provide designs 
and detailed construction documents that are fully compliant with all applicable codes, and that conflicts 
between Equinix standards and applicable codes are to be brought to our attention. 
 
Purpose 
This document defines the hardware selection to be used in Branch Circuit Monitoring systems (BCM) 
at all Equinix IBXs. 
 
Applicability 
This document applies to all Equinix IBX facilities and is to be used as reference for design elements of 
BCM and their selection and implementation.  It is supplementary to other applicable standards 
including, but not limited to, topology,  and global procurement. 
 
1.0   RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Global Operations Engineering shall maintain this standard with the assistance of the BCM stake 
holders. 
It is the responsibility of all Operations Engineering, Design Engineering, and Construction personnel to 
comply with this policy.   
It is the responsibility of the Manager Facilities Operations and/or Assistant Manager Facilities 
Operations to ensure that all Site Operations Engineering personnel are aware, understand, and have 
been trained on the implementation of this policy and its requirem
